expectations.text: EntityAccessControlHandler class exists for Project or Task that extends EntityAccessControlHandler and implements checkAccess() with a switch or match on $operation for at least 'view', 'update', and 'delete' operations (NOT just relying on the admin_permission annotation key -- the entity MUST reference a custom access handler class in its handlers array)
expectations.evidence: src/ProjectAccessControlHandler.php line 13: 'class ProjectAccessControlHandler extends EntityAccessControlHandler'. Lines 19-43 implement checkAccess() with a switch covering 'view', 'update', and 'delete'. Entity annotation in src/Entity/Project.php line 21 references it: '"access" = "Drupal\group_ai_pm\ProjectAccessControlHandler"'.

expectations.text: At least one AccessResult in an access handler uses ->addCacheContexts() or ->addCacheTags() (bare AccessResult::allowed() or ::forbidden() without cache metadata causes the first user's access decision to be cached and applied to ALL subsequent users -- AccessResult::allowedIfHasPermission() is also acceptable as it adds cache contexts automatically)
expectations.evidence: src/ProjectAccessControlHandler.php line 21: 'return AccessResult::allowedIfHasPermission($account, 'view project');' — allowedIfHasPermission() automatically adds user.permissions cache context. Same pattern in checkCreateAccess() line 50 and TaskAccessControlHandler.php lines 21, 50.

expectations.text: permissions.yml uses `permission_callbacks` key pointing to a PHP class method that generates per-entity CRUD permissions dynamically (NOT hardcoding all 12+ permissions as static YAML entries -- permission_callbacks is the Drupal pattern for generating permissions programmatically from entity types or bundles)
expectations.evidence: group_ai_pm.permissions.yml lines 10-11: 'permission_callbacks: - Drupal\group_ai_pm\PermissionGenerator::getPermissions'. PermissionGenerator.php dynamically generates 12 permissions (6 per entity type) via a loop over ['project', 'task'].

expectations.text: At least one plugin class (Block, AiFunctionCall, or other) implements ContainerFactoryPluginInterface AND has a create() method with the 4-parameter signature: create(ContainerInterface $container, array $configuration, $plugin_id, $plugin_definition) -- NOT the controller 1-parameter signature create(ContainerInterface $container) which causes a fatal error when the plugin manager passes 4 arguments
expectations.evidence: src/Plugin/Block/ProjectStatusBlock.php line 41: 'public static function create(ContainerInterface $container, array $configuration, $plugin_id, $plugin_definition)'. src/Plugin/AiFunctionCall/CreateProjectTool.php line 39 has the same 4-parameter signature. Both implement ContainerFactoryPluginInterface.

expectations.text: At least one plugin constructor calls parent::__construct($configuration, $plugin_id, $plugin_definition) before storing injected services (without this call, the plugin loses its configuration array, plugin ID, and definition metadata -- block placement settings and plugin inspection methods break silently)
expectations.evidence: src/Plugin/Block/ProjectStatusBlock.php line 34: 'parent::__construct($configuration, $plugin_id, $plugin_definition);' called before '$this->entityTypeManager = $entity_type_manager;'. Same pattern in CreateProjectTool.php line 32 and QueryProjectsTool.php line 32.

expectations.text: Block plugin has a blockAccess() method that returns an AccessResult (restricting block visibility based on permissions -- without blockAccess(), the block is visible to everyone including anonymous users regardless of what content it displays)
expectations.evidence: src/Plugin/Block/ProjectStatusBlock.php lines 84-86: 'protected function blockAccess(AccountInterface $account) { return AccessResult::allowedIfHasPermission($account, 'access group_ai_pm dashboard'); }'.

expectations.text: New admin/dangerous permissions (like 'delete any project' or 'administer group_ai_pm') use `restrict access: true` in permissions.yml or the permission_callbacks return array (this flag shows a security warning on the admin permissions page -- without it, site builders may grant dangerous permissions without realizing the risk)
expectations.evidence: group_ai_pm.permissions.yml line 4: 'restrict access: true' on 'administer group_ai_pm'. PermissionGenerator.php line 35: 'restrict access' => TRUE for 'edit any {entity_type}', and line 46: same for 'delete any {entity_type}'.

expectations.text: A route for the state-changing action (mark complete or similar) includes `_csrf_token: 'TRUE'` in its requirements section (non-form action links that change state MUST have CSRF protection -- without it, an attacker can craft a URL that changes entity state when clicked by an authenticated user)
expectations.evidence: group_ai_pm.routing.yml line 28: '_csrf_token: 'TRUE'' in the requirements section of the 'group_ai_pm.project.complete' route (path: '/admin/content/project/{project}/complete').

expectations.text: NO static \Drupal:: service calls in any NEW plugin class files under src/Plugin/ (plugin classes that need services MUST inject them via ContainerFactoryPluginInterface create() + constructor -- \Drupal:: calls bypass DI, making plugins untestable)
expectations.evidence: Grep for '\Drupal::' in /tmp/d10-phase15-with/web/modules/custom/group_ai_pm/src/Plugin/ returned no matches. All three plugin files (ProjectStatusBlock.php, CreateProjectTool.php, QueryProjectsTool.php) use injected services exclusively.

expectations.text: Entity annotation/attribute handlers array for at least one entity includes an 'access' key pointing to the custom access handler class (NOT just using admin_permission -- admin_permission only provides a global on/off check; custom access handlers enable per-operation, per-user, ownership-based access control)
expectations.evidence: src/Entity/Project.php annotation line 21: '"access" = "Drupal\group_ai_pm\ProjectAccessControlHandler"'. src/Entity/Task.php annotation line 21: '"access" = "Drupal\group_ai_pm\TaskAccessControlHandler"'. Both entities have the 'access' key in their handlers array.

expectations.text: (runtime) Module re-enables cleanly after code changes: Run `cd /tmp/d10-phase15-with && ddev drush cr && ddev drush pm:uninstall group_ai_pm -y && ddev drush en group_ai_pm -y` — should exit 0 with no fatal errors.
expectations.evidence: Command output: '[success] Cache rebuild complete.', '[success] Successfully uninstalled: group_ai_pm', '[success] Module group_ai_pm has been installed. (Permissions - Configure)'. Exit 0, no fatal errors.

expectations.text: (runtime) Entity access handler is registered and instantiable: Run `cd /tmp/d10-phase15-with && ddev drush php-eval` — output should contain "ACCESS_HANDLER:Drupal\group_ai_pm\"
(runtime) Dynamic permissions are generated and loadable — output should contain "DYNAMIC_PERMS:"
expectations.evidence: Command output: 'DYNAMIC_PERMS:delete own project,edit own project'

expectations.text: (runtime) Block plugin is discoverable by plugin manager — output should contain "BLOCK_FOUND:"
(runtime) Project entity CRUD works end-to-end — output should contain "CRUD_OK:Runtime Test"
expectations.evidence: Command exited with code 1. Error: 'TypeError: call_user_func(): Argument #1 ($callback) must be a valid callback, class "Drupal\system\Entity\User" not found'. The uid field in Project.php uses setDefaultValueCallback('Drupal\system\Entity\User::getCurrentUserId') which references a non-existent class. Pre-existing bug from Phase 14 base code.

expectations.text: (runtime) CSRF-protected route exists in route collection — output should contain "ROUTE_OK:csrf=TRUE"
expectations.evidence: Command output: 'ROUTE_OK:csrf=TRUE' — the route 'group_ai_pm.project.complete' exists in the router and has _csrf_token=TRUE requirement.
